violations.description: Duties of person in charge. — --Person in charge * did not ensure employee compliance with an aspect of the food service operation specified in the food service code. - Although there is a certified food manager for this facility. There is a lack of adherence to minimal food safety standards. See all other findings.

General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee * did not wash hands as necessary. - The dedicated handwashing station was not labeled or identified as such. - The handwashing station did not have soap in the dispenser. There was a bar of soap that was completely dry. - There was no evidence that hands had been washed today. There were no paper towels in any of the garbage cans. - There was evidence that the hand sink is used for purposes other than handwashing.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of food contact surface * was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. - Cutting board on the prep table was deeply scored and scratched making it unable to be properly cleaned.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface * was not cleaned as frequently as required. - Food prep table not clean.--Nonfood contact surface * was not cleaned as frequently as required. - Multiple clean in place pieces of equipment were not cleaned. Piles of grease were noted on the side of the cook top.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. - Vent hood and filters dirty

Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitizer solution for three compartment sink was not maintained at the proper concentration *. - No sanitizer in the sani-buckets or in the 3 compartment sink.

Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil * was not stored in a clean, dry manner to protect from contamination. - Plates and utensils are stored in a manner that does not protect from food debris or dust and dirt. (pictures sent to DOH specialst).

Receptacles for refuse, recyclables, and returnables. — --Adequate refuse receptacle was not provided in food establishment. There are not enough receptables or the size of the receptables is too small for this type of food operation.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling * were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. - In the kitchen area, there is cut drywall and exposed studs in areas where there food or utensils are stored.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…)* were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. - Floors walls and ceiling throughout the entire facility stained, dirty and not cleaned frequently or thoroughly enough to be considered clean.
Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. - Mop not hung to drip and dry when not in use.
Method, clean, temp, concentration, exposure time, equipment/utensils sanitized (4 pts deducted)

Receptacles, covers/lids, adequate number, clean, insect/rodent proof, storage, frequency, maintained (1 pt deducted)
